page: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 48 vCPUs and 48 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 5.33 GB of memory per core, alongside 2048 GB of local SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile characterized by strong multi-threaded capabilities but limited single-core throughput. The server demonstrates poor single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the bottom 10%, and weak single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-threaded operations are significantly stronger, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25% and passmark CPU encryption tests also ranking in the top 25%. Most other metrics, including Geekbench multi-core, static web serving, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average range.

This server is suited for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage its 48 cores, such as multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications due to its lower single-core clock speed and shared CPU allocation. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high core count and substantial 256 GB memory footprint combined with 2 TB of local SSD storage offer a dense resource profile for multi-tenant environments or parallel batch processing without the premium cost of dedicated CPU instances.
description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based general-purpose virtual server configured with 48 vCPUs and 48 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, 2048 GB of local SSD storage, and a shared CPU allocation. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, whereas multi-threaded performance is robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and Passmark encryption benchmarks scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth, static web serving, and Redis SET operations perform at average levels. This server is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving where resource density is prioritized over peak single-core performance.
og_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2048 GB local SSD, offering strong multi-threaded performance alongside average web serving capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2 TB SSD, optimized for parallel multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density virtual server featuring forty-eight cores and two terabytes of local SSD storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 48 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Includes 2048 GB of local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Strong multi-threaded performance, ranking in the top 25% for bzip3 decompression
Lower single-core performance, placing in the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks
Well-suited for parallel data processing, software compilation, and web serving
